title: ProofHub vs Bitrix24 for Non-technical users
meta_description: ProofHub vs Bitrix24 for non-technical users who want simple project tasks without CRM dashboards or complex modules.
constraint_lens: You want straightforward project tasks without CRM modules or complex dashboards that make you unsure where work lives.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for non-technical users who need simple project task tracking.
one_second_verdict.reason: Bitrix24 fails first because cRM modules and complex dashboards introduce uncertainty about where tasks live.

verdict.summary: ProofHub wins for non-technical users who want simple project task tracking. It centers on tasks, boards, and basic project views without CRM layers. Bitrix24 includes CRM modules, sales pipelines, and multi-panel dashboards that can feel overwhelming. If CRM modules and complex dashboards introduce uncertainty about where tasks live, Bitrix24 fails first.
verdict.decision_rule: If CRM modules and complex dashboards introduce uncertainty about where tasks live, Bitrix24 fails first.

sections.heading: Best fit for straightforward project tracking
sections.content: You need straightforward project tasks and avoid CRM-style dashboards. ProofHub opens to projects with task lists and boards as the main focus. Bitrix24 combines tasks with CRM pipelines, deals, and multiple dashboard widgets that can blur where simple tasks belong.

bullets.point: Task lists and kanban boards as primary views
bullets.why_it_matters: You see projects organized around tasks instead of navigating sales or CRM panels.

bullets.point: Clear project spaces with files and discussions
bullets.why_it_matters: Work items stay grouped inside projects without mixing with deal pipelines.

bullets.point: Limited module surface compared to CRM suites
bullets.why_it_matters: You are less likely to click into unfamiliar sections that feel unrelated to task tracking.

bullets.point: Integrated CRM with deals and pipelines
bullets.why_it_matters: You manage sales and customer data alongside tasks, though this adds extra panels.
You track different metrics in one place, but must understand what each widget represents.

bullets.point: Wide feature set including communication tools
bullets.why_it_matters: You consolidate many business functions, which can increase complexity.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: You need full CRM pipelines and advanced business modules in the same system.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Bitrix24 if customer relationship management is central to your workflow.

items.fails_when: You open the dashboard and cannot tell whether to use tasks, deals, or another module.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to ProofHub for a task-centered interface.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If your projects are closely tied to sales pipelines and you are comfortable navigating CRM sections, Bitrix24 may be appropriate.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: If you want task-focused projects without CRM layers, choose ProofHub.
If dashboards with deals and pipelines feel confusing, avoid Bitrix24.
If you need tasks tightly integrated with CRM data, consider Bitrix24.

faqs.a: Yes. It includes deal pipelines, contacts, and sales modules alongside tasks.

faqs.q: Is ProofHub focused mainly on tasks?
faqs.a: Yes. Its core interface centers on projects, task lists, and boards.

faqs.q: Which is easier for non-technical users?
faqs.a: ProofHub is generally easier because it avoids CRM-style dashboards.

faqs.q: When would Bitrix24 be a better choice?
faqs.a: It makes sense if you need tasks deeply integrated with sales and customer management.
